title: Sovrn Commerce CTR tracking and GitHub deploy

metadata.reconstruction_note: No fresh verbatim codex-chat-transcript.json export was available on disk; this snapshot was reconstructed from the active Codex thread context before upload.

messages.text: Asked to update Sovrn Commerce controls in the frontend, after first making sure the repo had the latest website from git.
Fetched origin/main, preserved existing local changes in a stash, fast-forwarded main to origin/main, and reapplied local changes. Confirmed the repo was updated before frontend edits.

Read Sovrn Commerce JavaScript docs. Implemented config-driven Sovrn setup in config.js and index.html using window.SOVRN_COMMERCE, vglnk config, vglnk.js loading, norewrite/nooptimize footer links, sponsored product links, and optional vglnk.open click handling.
Added a Playwright test that product cards prefer plain merchant URLs and include rel=sponsored. Verified npm run test:e2e passed.
Asked how to track click-through rate with Sovrn Commerce and where to put the API key.

Explained that Sovrn tracks clicks once their Commerce JavaScript is installed with the campaign key, and that CTR also needs a site-side denominator such as product card impressions/views. Recommended config.js for the public JavaScript key and server-side env/secrets for the API secret.
Asked to implement CTR tracking.

Added product_card_events migration with event_type enum, table, RLS insert policy, service-role read policy, indexes, and product_card_ctr_daily view. Pushed migration 20260428000000 to the linked Supabase FWM project. Added frontend tracking for product card impressions and clicks, tying events to image_id, search_event_id, anon/session IDs, product URL, source site, card position, and random/search context. Updated database.types.ts and Playwright coverage. Verified npm run test:e2e passed.
Asked to apply it and where to put Sovrn Commerce API key and API secret.

Applied the CTR migration to Supabase. Added SOVRN_COMMERCE_API_SECRET placeholder to .env.example and explained public JavaScript campaign key belongs in config.js while the API secret should stay server-side.
Asked to add empty string variables in config.js for the key and secret.

Added window.SOVRN_COMMERCE_API_KEY = "" and window.SOVRN_COMMERCE_API_SECRET = "" to config.js, and wired window.SOVRN_COMMERCE.key to the public key variable. Warned that config.js is browser-visible.
Added key and secret to config.js and asked how to track views too, referencing the Sovrn Commerce JavaScript page.

Re-read Sovrn docs and concluded Sovrn Commerce JavaScript documentation is click-oriented and does not expose a product-card view event for normal cards. Added our own view event, distinct from render impressions, using IntersectionObserver at 50% visibility in the results pane. Added migration 20260428010000 to extend enum with view and update product_card_ctr_daily to include views and view_click_through_rate. Fixed Postgres migration issues by comparing enum values as text and recreating the view. Pushed the migration to Supabase. Updated tests and verified npm run test:e2e passed.
Asked whether card views can be associated with measurements input by the user.

Explained that product_card_events.search_event_id links search-result impressions/views/clicks to search_events, which stores measurement inputs such as feet, inches, weight_lb, bust_in, hips_in, and clothing_type. Homepage random cards have search_event_id null because no search exists yet.
Asked whether search-result card views can be distinguished from random homepage card views.

Explained product_card_events.result_context is set to random for homepage cards and search for post-search cards, and gave example SQL for grouping views/clicks by result_context and joining search views to search_events.

Shared a Sovrn dashboard screenshot showing CTR and Views cards locked with “Install the JavaScript on your website to unlock additional insights,” and asked whether this was caching or the test had not passed.

Inspected config.js without printing secret values. Found the public key and secret were present but window.SOVRN_COMMERCE.enabled was still false, meaning Sovrn JS would abort. Set enabled to true, verified tests passed, and explained the dashboard likely has not detected active Commerce JavaScript yet. Also noted the screenshot date range ended 2026-04-27 while today is 2026-04-28, so current test traffic would not show.
Asked to push everything done here to GitHub so the changes are live, and then save the transcript and summary to Supabase transcripts table.

Redacted the Sovrn API secret from config.js before committing because config.js is public/browser-served. Staged only the Sovrn/CTR files, leaving unrelated .gitignore and non-Amazon scraper files unstaged. Committed as 2e7b1d3 Add Sovrn Commerce and product card tracking after precommit Playwright tests passed, and pushed main to GitHub origin. Then created this transcript snapshot for upload because no fresh verbatim transcript export existed on disk.
Asked to deploy after Sovrn Commerce dashboard still did not show vglnk.js in Chrome DevTools.

Confirmed README says Cloudflare Pages auto-deploys from GitHub main. Verified the live site was serving the pushed Sovrn/CTR code, including config.js with key and enabled true, the homepage Sovrn loader, and product_card_events tracking. A clean Playwright runtime saw cdn.viglink.com/api/vglnk.js load with status 200, but also saw api.viglink.com/api/ping CORS noise.
Reported not seeing vglnk.js in Chrome DevTools and shared a screenshot.

Moved the Sovrn loader into an inline script immediately after config.js, closer to Sovrn documented install snippet, while preserving module code for CUID/session configuration. Ran npm run test:e2e successfully, committed d6a94b9 Use inline Sovrn Commerce loader, pushed to GitHub main, confirmed Cloudflare served the new snippet, and verified live Chromium requested https://cdn.viglink.com/api/vglnk.js with status 200 and api.viglink.com/api/ping fired.
Confirmed the Sovrn dashboard is fixed with CTR and Views showing, and asked to update the transcripts table.
